title: BEAM Therapeutics Base Editing AATD Clinical Trial Results
text: Beam Therapeutics announced updated clinical results for BEAM-302, a base editing treatment for alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency (AATD). The Phase 1/2 trial involved 29 patients across three dose groups, with follow-up periods ranging from approximately one to 12 months. All three patient groups achieved functional protein levels exceeding a protective threshold for liver and lung safety. The treatment reduced circulating misfolded protein levels by more than 80% on average. According to the company, this approach represents the first one that both gives the liver the ability to produce the right type of AAT and reduces the toxic accumulation of mutated proteins. Two patients experienced elevated liver enzyme levels, though both remained asymptomatic and required no intervention. Beam selected the 60 mg single-dose regimen for advancement and plans to expand the trial later in 2026. The company intends to pursue an accelerated approval pathway with the FDA. The announcement intensifies competition in AATD treatment development, with other biotechs including Wave Life Sciences and Airna also advancing their own approaches to the rare disease.
